title: helianthemum canum subsp. ciscaucasicum
intro: Helianthemum canum subsp. ciscaucasicum, also known as Caucasian rockrose, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Cistaceae. It is native to the Caucasus region and is used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Herbal preparations of Caucasian rockrose include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to promote wound healing and alleviate various health conditions.
